assertions.evidence: Multiple markdown tables present, starting with the main table '| Scenario | Bulk Discount | Loyalty Discount | Final Discount? | Open Questions |'

assertions.text: The output does NOT silently resolve the stacking/cap ambiguity — it leaves the conflicting cases as open questions, blank cells, or explicitly marks them as unresolved
assertions.evidence: All rows where both discounts apply have '?' in Final Discount? column. The response explicitly states 'The rows marked ? all depend on one unresolved policy decision that the team needs to make' and presents Options A, B, C as alternatives without choosing one.

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row where both bulk and loyalty discounts apply simultaneously (exposing the interaction)
assertions.evidence: Multiple rows with both discounts non-zero: 'Both apply — stacked' (20%, 15%), 'Both apply — higher only' (20%, 15%), 'Both apply, loyalty is higher' (10%, 15%), 'Both apply, equal discounts' (15%, 15%)

assertions.text: The unresolved decision (stacking vs. higher-only vs. cap) is explicitly surfaced — via an Open Questions column, a '?' cell, or a note
assertions.evidence: Table has an 'Open Questions' column with per-row notes (e.g. 'Stacked = 35%? Or compounded = 32%?', 'Higher only = 20%?', 'What is the cap? 25%? 30%?'). Also a dedicated 'The Open Decision' section naming all three options explicitly.

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: 'Final Discount?' column ends with '?' in all tables
